title: EXPLAIN output not working/ not similar to docs
description:
### What happens?

When I tried to use the `EXPLAIN` keyword the output does not give me any numbers or anything that looks similar to what is shown in the docs.

### To Reproduce

When I try to reproduce the example in https://duckdb.org/docs/dev/profiling.html

```python
import duckdb

con = duckdb.connect(database=":memory:")

query = """
SET explain_output = 'all';

CREATE TABLE students (name VARCHAR, sid INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE exams (eid INTEGER, subject VARCHAR, sid INTEGER);
INSERT INTO students VALUES ('Mark', 1), ('Joe', 2), ('Matthew', 3);
INSERT INTO exams VALUES (10, 'Physics', 1), (20, 'Chemistry', 2), (30, 'Literature', 3);
EXPLAIN SELECT name FROM students JOIN exams USING (sid) WHERE name LIKE 'Ma%';
"""

data = con.sql(query=query).df()
print(data)
```

this is what I see

```bash
     explain_key                                      explain_value
0   logical_plan  ┌───────────────────────────┐                 ...
1    logical_opt  ┌───────────────────────────┐                 ...
2  physical_plan  ┌───────────────────────────┐                 ...
```

This is not at all what I see in the docs

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/495cc170-a7f5-45bb-b708-1a32ccafd582)


### OS:

linux, x86

### DuckDB Version:

??

### DuckDB Client:

duckdb==1.0.0 in python

### Full Name:

Marc Skov Madsen

### Affiliation:

Ørsted

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE courses (course_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, course_name VARCHAR NOT NULL, credits INTEGER CHECK (credits > 0));
CREATE TABLE enrollments (enrollment_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, sid INTEGER, course_id INTEGER, FOREIGN KEY (sid) REFERENCES students(sid), FOREIGN KEY (course_id) REFERENCES courses(course_id));
SELECT c.course_name FROM courses c JOIN enrollments e ON c.course_id = e.course_id WHERE e.sid IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: EXPLAIN output does not match documentation expectations
